page: Amazon Web Services m5a.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz and includes dedicated allocation. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate capability profile. Single-core processing is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 10%. Multi-core performance is slightly stronger but remains modest, with stress-ng multi-core and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falling into the bottom 25%. However, the instance achieves average tier results in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is average, though large uncached read and write operations drop to the bottom 25%.

In terms of qualitative cost efficiency, the m5a.4xlarge leverages AMD EPYC hardware to offer a balanced resource density of compute and memory for general-purpose workloads without the premium of high-frequency compute tiers. It is well-suited for applications that require moderate, multi-threaded processing and balanced memory, such as Redis caching, standard database operations, and software compilation. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks, heavy text processing, or large-scale LLM inference, where its performance is constrained.
description: Amazon Web Services m5a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows that while single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks are limited, multi-core execution and memory bandwidth for cached blocks perform at an average level. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring balanced resource allocation rather than peak single-thread speed. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, Redis caching, and standard database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 processor, optimized for balanced workloads under the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 processor on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring AMD EPYC processors and dedicated memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB DDR4 RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz
Delivers 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Average performance in multi-core benchmarks, software compilation, and Redis operations
Best suited for general-purpose applications, caching, and standard databases
